summary: Ukraine reports multiple Shahed drone swarms targeting southern regions and a large UAV strike on Crimea and Sevastopol. Russian sources claim the ability to launch up to 100 ballistic missiles per month and warn of Iskander-M launches from Bryansk. Both sides continue using UAVs and missiles across the front lines.
key_points: Ukraine’s intelligence reports over 20 drones shot down over Sevastopol and a deadly UAV attack on Melitopol, killing civilians (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30663).
Russia claims it can launch up to 100 ballistic missiles monthly and produce 55‑60 missiles per month, planning up to 60 Kinzhal hypersonic missiles this year (Source: @UkraineNow/81671).
High threat alert issued for possible Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67473).

Shahed drone swarms observed: 13 heading toward Fountain/Odessa, 6 over Mykolaiv toward Odesa, 5 over the Black Sea from Kherson, and 2 over Dnipro (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67483, @mon1tor_ua/67481, @mon1tor_ua/67478).
Russian UAV “Geranium” struck the Nikopol power plant in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @tass_agency/379031).
Reactive drone detected on the north of Kyiv moving along the Belarus border toward Zhytomyr (Source: @kpszsu/63957).
Multiple guided UAVs reported near Sumy, Kyiv, and Chernihiv regions heading toward strategic towns such as Shostka, Shostka, and Ichnya (Source: @kpszsu/63939).

sections.executive_summary: Russia asserts capability to launch up to 100 ballistic missiles per month and warns of Iskander‑M launches from Bryansk. Ukraine reports extensive UAV attacks on Crimea, Sevastopol, and Melitopol, causing civilian casualties. Shahed drone swarms continue to target southern Ukrainian regions, intensifying the air threat.

points: Ukrainian intelligence documented a massive UAV assault on Crimea and Sevastopol during the night, with more than 20 drones downed over Sevastopol and three killed in Simferopol (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30663).
In Melitopol, a civilian car was hit on the highway between Novonikolayka and Trudovoe, killing the driver and passenger, and a school attack injured six civilians (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30663).
Shahed drone activity was reported over Mykolaiv heading toward Odesa and over the Black Sea from Kherson, indicating coordinated southern air attacks (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67481).
Ukrainian forces reported guided UAVs operating near Sumy, Kyiv, and Chernihiv, targeting towns such as Shostka, Ichnya, and approaching the border with Belarus (Source: @kpszsu/63939).
A reactive drone was observed on the north of Kyiv moving along the Belarus border toward Zhytomyr, suggesting potential reconnaissance or strike intent (Source: @kpszsu/63957).

Russian officials state the country can field up to 100 ballistic missiles each month, maintaining a steady production rate of 55‑60 missiles and planning delivery of up to 60 Kinzhal hypersonic missiles this year (Source: @UkraineNow/81671).
A high alert was issued for possible Iskander‑M missile launches from the Bryansk region, highlighting a heightened ballistic threat to Ukrainian territory (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67473).

The Russian military reported that a UAV designated “Geranium” successfully struck the Nikopol power plant in Dnipropetrovsk region, demonstrating continued use of strike drones against infrastructure (Source: @tass_agency/379031).

Multiple guided UAVs were reported operating in the north of Sumy region, moving past Shostka and heading toward strategic locations, indicating Russian reconnaissance and strike activity (Source: @kpszsu/63939).

Ukrainian forces engaged in infantry battles in the Shostka district of Sumy, advancing up to 800 meters in eleven sectors and continuing fights in Ivolzhansk and Pisarevka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30663).
Russian assault units of the “North” group advanced toward Kachiy Lopan and Okhrimovka on the Kharkiv direction, indicating ongoing ground offensives (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30663).
Guided UAVs were observed near the Sumy region heading toward Shostka and Ichnya, suggesting Russian aerial pressure on eastern supply routes (Source: @kpszsu/63939).
In the Zaporozhye region, battles for Komsomolskoe (Gulyaypole) were reported, reflecting continued fighting on the eastern front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30663).
Ukrainian reports of guided UAVs operating near Chernihiv, passing Bakhmach toward Ichnya, indicate Russian aerial activity in the north (Source: @kpszsu/63939).
A reactive drone was detected on the north of Kyiv moving along the Belarus border toward Zhytomyr, highlighting potential northern infiltration routes (Source: @kpszsu/63957).
Ukrainian forces reported infantry battles near Bachevsk and surrounding settlements on the Sumy direction, part of the northern operational area (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30663).
Russian UAV activity near the north of Sumy, heading toward Shostka, suggests continued pressure on northern logistics (Source: @kpszsu/63939).

Shahed drone swarms of 13 units were observed heading toward Fountain/Odessa, and additional swarms of 6 and 5 units over Mykolaiv and the Black Sea from Kherson, indicating intensified southern air attacks (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67483, @mon1tor_ua/67481).

Ukrainian reports of a civilian car hit on the highway between Novonikolayka and Trudovoe in the Melitopol district resulted in two fatalities, showing ground-level threats in the south (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30663).
A school in Melitopol was attacked, injuring six civilians, highlighting the vulnerability of civilian infrastructure in the southern region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30663).
Russian UAV “Geranium” strike on the Nikopol power plant in Dnipropetrovsk region demonstrates the use of drones against critical energy infrastructure in the south (Source: @tass_agency/379031).
Ukrainian intelligence reported over 20 drones shot down over Sevastopol and a lethal UAV attack on Crimea, indicating heavy aerial combat in the Black Sea region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30663).
Shahed drone swarms targeting Odessa, Mykolaiv, and the Black Sea were observed, reflecting ongoing low‑altitude UAV threats to Ukrainian air defenses (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67483, @mon1tor_ua/67481).
Russian UAV “Geranium” successfully struck the Nikopol power plant, showing capability to conduct precision strikes against strategic assets (Source: @tass_agency/379031).
Multiple guided UAVs were reported near Sumy, Kyiv, and Chernihiv, moving toward key towns, indicating persistent Russian aerial reconnaissance and strike missions (Source: @kpszsu/63939).

key_developments: Ukraine’s intelligence documented a massive UAV assault on Crimea and Sevastopol, with over 20 drones downed and civilian casualties in Simferopol (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30663).
Russia claims the ability to launch up to 100 ballistic missiles per month and plans to deliver up to 60 Kinzhal hypersonic missiles this year (Source: @UkraineNow/81671).
A high alert was issued for potential Iskander‑M missile launches from Bryansk region, indicating a heightened ballistic threat (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67473).

Shahed drone swarms of 13, 6, 5, and 2 units were observed targeting Odessa, Mykolaiv, the Black Sea, and Dnipro, respectively, showing intensified southern UAV attacks (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67483, @mon1tor_ua/67481, @mon1tor_ua/67478).
Russian UAV “Geranium” struck the Nikopol power plant in Dnipropetrovsk region, demonstrating continued use of strike drones against infrastructure (Source: @tass_agency/379031).
A reactive drone was detected on the north of Kyiv moving along the Belarus border toward Zhytomyr, suggesting possible reconnaissance or strike intent (Source: @kpszsu/63957).
Ukrainian forces reported infantry advances of up to 800 meters in eleven sectors of the Sumy direction and ongoing battles in Ivolzhansk and Pisarevka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30663).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian ballistic missile production and the potential Iskander‑M launches pose a strategic long‑range threat to Ukrainian command centers and critical infrastructure. The proliferation of Shahed and other UAV swarms across the southern front strains Ukrainian air defenses and endangers civilian populations. Russian guided UAV activity in the north and east indicates continued reconnaissance and precision strike capabilities, complicating Ukrainian ground operations.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is reinforcing air defense assets in the south, particularly around Odessa and Mykolaiv, while mobilizing infantry to exploit modest gains in the Sumy sector. International partners are monitoring the reported Russian missile capacity, which may influence future sanctions and aid packages. NATO statements underscore political support but operational assistance remains focused on counter‑UAV systems and artillery replenishment.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source monitoring continues to track UAV flight paths, confirming multiple Shahed launch corridors from Russian‑held territories. The reported production rates of ballistic missiles suggest Russia can sustain high‑tempo missile strikes, necessitating enhanced early‑warning radar coverage. Ukrainian reports of guided UAVs near key logistical hubs highlight the need for integrated electronic warfare measures to disrupt enemy drone command and control.
